input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode (latest stable). After installing, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- Open Xcode → **Settings → Platforms** → ensure an iOS Simulator runtime is downloaded.
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version` and `xcrun simctl list devices`

---

## 2. .NET SDK (includes MAUI workload)

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest .NET SDK (currently .NET 9).
- Use the official installer (`.pkg`) or the install script:
  ```
  brew install --cask dotnet-sdk
  ```
- **Discover versions:** `dotnet --list-sdks` and check [dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy) for supported versions.

---

## 3. MAUI Workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` (and its sub-workloads like `maui-ios`, `maui-android`).

---

## 4. Android SDK & Java JDK

The MAUI workload installs the **Android SDK** and a compatible **JDK (Microsoft OpenJDK)** automatically via the `maui-android` workload. If you need to manage them manually:

- **Android:** Install Android Studio from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio), then use SDK Manager to install:
  - Android SDK Platform (API level matching your `TargetFramework`, e.g., API 35 for `net9.0-android`)
  - Android SDK Build-Tools
  - Android SDK Command-line Tools
  - Android Emulator + a system image
- **JDK:** Microsoft OpenJDK 17 — bundled with the workload, or get it from [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download)
- Set environment variables if using a manual install:
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  export JAVA_HOME="/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/microsoft-17.jdk/Contents/Home"
  ```
- **Verify:** `dotnet android sdk info` or check `~/.android/` and `$ANDROID_HOME/platforms/`

---

## 5. IDE (pick one)

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** (full MAUI support) | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been retired — VS Code or Rider are the recommended options.

---

## 6. Verify the Full Toolchain

```bash
# Check .NET and workloads
dotnet --info
dotnet workload list

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for each platform
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

---

## Quick Troubleshooting

| Problem | Fix |
|---|---|
| `xcode-select` errors | `sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app` |
| Missing Android SDK | `dotnet workload repair` or install via Android Studio SDK Manager |
| Workload restore issues | `dotnet workload update` then `dotnet workload repair` |
| Provisioning/signing (real iOS device) | Configure in Xcode → Accounts, or set `<CodesignKey>` in `.csproj` |

---

**Install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → IDE → verify. The workload handles most Android dependencies for you.
